sections.paragraph:
παραλυτικός, ή, όν (παραλύω; Diosc. 1, 16; Vett. Val. 110, 34; 127, 21; Hippiatr. I 433, 6; Papiri letterari greci, ed. ACarlini et al. ’78, no. 32, 17: New Docs 3, 79; Just., A I, 22, 6) lame only subst. (ὁ) π. lame person, paralytic (Rufus [II a.d.] in Oribas. 8, 39, 8; Geopon. 8, 11) Mt 4:24; 8:6; 9:2ab, 6 (on Mt 8 and 9 s. JKingsbury, CBQ 40, ’79, 559–73); Mk 2:3–5, 9f; Lk 5:24 v.l.; J 5:3 D; AcPl [Ha 8, 37] //BMM verso, 11 παρ[αλυτι]κοὺς ἐγ[είρων].—PSchmidt, Die Geschichte Jesu II 1904, 205ff; 261.—DELG s.v. λύω. M-M.

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

Hippiatr. Hippiatr. = Corpus hippiatricorum Graecorum, IX a.d.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Geopon Geopon , a collection of older material, X a.d.—List 5

CBQ CBQ = Catholic Biblical Quarterly—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 768
